titleStmt.title: SII 18.262: original edition by N. Lakshminarayan Rao
No. 262. (B.K. No. 9 of 1934-35.) BENKANAKOṆḌA, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.hi: Mahā-sati stone set up near the southern entrance into the Kalamēśvara temple.

summary.p: This inscription belonging to the reign of Rāmachandradēvarāya is dated in the 15th year of his reign, Tāraṇa, Bhādrapada śu. 10, Monday, corresponding to A.D. 1284, August 21, Monday, f.d.t. ·30.
It states that Chaṭaya-Nāyaka of Muḷugunda died with his wife to keep the word given to their master, for which act, the kammata of Beḷugali was bestowed upon their family for ever.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
© svasti śrīmatu yādavanārāyaṇaṁ bhujabaḷaprauḍhapratāpaca
kravartti śrīvīrarāya rāmacaṁdradēvarāya rājyōdayada 15 tā
raṇasauvatsarada . bhādrapada sudha 10 sō . svasti śrīmatu muḷaguṁda
da naralōkavīra jagadāḷa bomidēva jaṁguḷimaṁneyaragaṁḍage [E]kaṭiga caṭaya
nāyakanu taṁna keṁḍatisahita yibaruṁ voḍeyaṁge koṭṭabhāsege
talegoṭṭaru yadakke meci beḷugaliya kaṁmatavanu makkaḷu makkaḷu dapade
koṭṭaru . bhūmivu caṁdranu vuḷḷanabara . yidakke Āḷupidaḍe vāraṇā

siyali ka(ṁ)vileya koṁda dōsa . svadataṁ paradataṁ vāpnōti . maṁgaḷa ma
hāśrī śrī śrī ©
Digital edition of SII 18.262 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
